page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 8 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 474 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench text processing also show strong performance, placing in the top 25%. However, the instance exhibits a weak result in the Passmark CPU encryption test (bottom 25%) and poor performance in LLM inference prompt processing for medium 7B models (bottom 10%). Most other compute and memory bandwidth benchmarks, including Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput, fall within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8gd.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density by combining dedicated ARMv9 cores with high-speed local NVMe storage. This makes it a viable option for workloads requiring fast local scratch space without the added overhead of network-attached storage. The instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, text processing, database operations, and lightweight web serving, though it is less optimal for heavy cryptographic tasks or large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an 8-core ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps, and a bundled 474 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers dedicated single-threaded performance per core. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top 10%, alongside strong single-threaded text processing and bzip3 decompression. However, it shows weaker results in CPU encryption and medium-sized LLM prompt processing. Qualitatively, the inclusion of high-speed local NVMe storage provides excellent resource density for data-intensive tasks. The m8gd.2xlarge is best suited for general-purpose applications, database operations, text processing, and web serving workloads that benefit from fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.2xlarge is an ARMv9 Graviton-powered general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated cores, 32 GB RAM, and a bundled 474 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.2xlarge is an ARMv9 Graviton instance with 8 cores, 32 GB RAM, and 474 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARMv9 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated cores, top-tier memory performance, and high-speed bundled NVMe SSD local storage.

Includes 32.0 GB of system memory with top-tier memory benchmark performance
